description: Fetches federal grant opportunities from Grants.gov daily, filters by nonprofit focus areas, funding ceiling, and deadline, generates personalized fit blurbs via OpenAI, and posts a curated digest to an internal Slack webhook endpoint.

trigger.description: Weekdays at 7am Eastern (DST-aware via America/New_York timezone)

steps.name: Fetch Grants - Workforce Development
steps.description: Calls the Grants.gov API v2 SEARCH endpoint for workforce development focus area.

body.query: workforce development OR job training OR employment

output_shape.raw_response: Grants.gov v2 API response for workforce focus area

steps.error_handling: On failure after 3 retries, route to workflow failure alert step

steps.name: Fetch Grants - Rural Broadband
steps.description: Fetches rural broadband focus area grants from Grants.gov API v2.

body.query: rural broadband OR broadband connectivity OR internet access

output_shape.raw_response: Grants.gov v2 API response for rural broadband focus area

steps.error_handling: On failure after 3 retries, route to workflow failure alert step

steps.name: Fetch Grants - Veteran Services
steps.description: Fetches veteran services focus area grants from Grants.gov API v2.

body.query: veteran services OR veterans OR military

output_shape.raw_response: Grants.gov v2 API response for veteran services focus area

steps.error_handling: On failure after 3 retries, route to workflow failure alert step

steps.name: Fetch Grants - Early Childhood Education
steps.description: Fetches early childhood education focus area grants from Grants.gov API v2.

body.query: early childhood education OR preschool OR head start OR child care

output_shape.raw_response: Grants.gov v2 API response for early childhood education focus area

steps.error_handling: On failure after 3 retries, route to workflow failure alert step

steps.name: Merge and Deduplicate API Responses
steps.description: Receives all 4 focus-area API responses stored sequentially on the item stream (each prior HTTP node passes its result forward; this Code node reads all items via $input.all()). Deduplicates by oppNumber/id, validates the response envelope shape, and asserts that at least one expected schema field is present. If the response envelope is unrecognized across all inputs, throws an error routing to failure. Also detects the case where all four queries return 0 hits and flags it as a potential query-parameter mismatch. NOTE: Because the Build Agent wires nodes linearly, the four httpRequest nodes feed into this Code node sequentially. $input.all() will contain the items passed through from the chain; the Code node reads each item's json as a separate API response.

output_shape.totalRawCount: total hits across all 4 API responses before dedup
output_shape.envelopeUnrecognizedCount: number of API responses with unrecognized envelope
output_shape.firstGrantKeys: comma-separated field names of first grant for debugging

steps.error_handling: Throws on total envelope parse failure or all-zero hits across all queries, routing to failure alert

steps.name: Filter and Classify Grants by Hard Criteria
steps.description: Parses the merged Grants.gov response, filters grants by focus area keyword match, funding ceiling >= $250,000, and deadline >= 21 days from today. Includes schema-validation assertions: throws if ALL grants have ceiling=0 AND daysLeft=-1 (full schema failure). If ALL deadlines are invalid but ceilings are readable, sets deadlineSchemaWarning flag and includes a soft-alert in the output payload instead of routing to the failure path.

output_shape.qualifying: array of grants passing all 3 criteria
output_shape.borderline: array of grants passing 2 of 3 criteria
output_shape.deadlineSchemaWarning: null or warning string if all deadlines are unreadable but ceilings are readable

steps.error_handling: Throws on full schema parse failure (all-zero ceiling + all-invalid deadline), routing to failure alert. Partial schema drift (deadline only) sets deadlineSchemaWarning and continues.

steps.name: Check If Any Qualifying Grants Found
steps.description: Routes to blurb generation if qualifying grants exist, otherwise routes to empty digest path. step_3 false-branch is explicitly wired to step_9.

steps.name: Generate OpenAI Fit Blurbs for Qualifying Grants
steps.description: Iterates over qualifying grants (capped at 10) and calls OpenAI GPT-4o-mini for each using the $http helper (supported in n8n Code nodes) to generate a personalized 2-3 sentence blurb. Includes a 200ms delay between calls and specific handling for HTTP 429 rate-limit responses with a single retry after 2s. Tracks blurb source ('openai' vs 'fallback'). If ALL blurbs fall back to template, sets allBlurbsFailed=true for the quality gate.

output_shape.qualifying: array of grants (up to 10) with blurb and blurbSource fields added
output_shape.fallbackCount: number of blurbs from fallback template
output_shape.allBlurbsFailed: true if ALL blurbs used fallback (total OpenAI failure)

steps.error_handling: Individual blurb failures fall back to template text and increment fallbackCount; 429s retried once after 2s; overall node failure routes to failure alert

steps.name: Validate Blurbs and Decide Quality Gate Flag
steps.description: Checks blurbs for minimum length (>50 chars). If ALL blurbs fell back to template (allBlurbsFailed=true), routes to a WARNING-level soft path rather than a hard ERROR: sets blurbsValid=false with alertLevel=WARNING so the digest is still posted with fallback blurbs and a clear warning, rather than treating OpenAI outage the same as a data pipeline failure. Sets blurbsValid=true only when at least one OpenAI blurb succeeded and all blurbs meet the 50-char threshold.

output_shape.blurbsValid: boolean - false only if individual blurbs are too short; true even when all blurbs are fallback (soft warning path)
output_shape.blurbAlertLevel: null or WARNING if all blurbs used fallback
output_shape.useBlurbFallbackPath: true if all blurbs are fallback (OpenAI down)

steps.name: Check Blurb Quality Gate
steps.description: Routes to digest formatting if blurbs are valid (including when all are fallback with WARNING), otherwise routes to failure alert for hard blurb validation failures (individual blurbs below threshold).

output_shape.true_branch: blurbs valid (or fallback with warning), proceed to format
output_shape.false_branch: hard blurb validation failure, alert team

steps.name: Format Full Digest Payload
steps.description: Assembles the final JSON digest payload with summary, qualifying grant items (agency, ceiling, deadline, link, blurb, blurbSource), and borderline grants flagged separately. Includes warning flags if partial or full fallback blurbs were used. Also propagates deadlineSchemaWarning if present.

output_shape.items: array of formatted qualifying grants with blurbSource
output_shape.borderline: array of borderline grants with missed criteria noted
output_shape.blurbWarning: null or warning string if partial or full fallback blurbs used
output_shape.alertLevel: null or WARNING if all blurbs were fallback
output_shape.deadlineSchemaWarning: null or schema drift warning string

steps.name: Post Digest to Internal Slack Endpoint
steps.description: POSTs the formatted JSON digest to the internal Slack-poster HTTP endpoint.

output_shape.status: HTTP response status from Slack endpoint

steps.error_handling: On failure after 3 retries, route to failure alert (step_8 failure skips step_10b so counter is NOT reset on failed delivery - intentional)

steps.name: Reset Consecutive Empty Days Counter
steps.description: Resets the consecutiveEmptyDays counter in n8n static data after a successful qualifying digest run (step_8 succeeded). Only fires on the qualifying path after confirmed Slack delivery success. A step_8 failure routes to step_11 instead, intentionally skipping this reset so the counter is only cleared on confirmed delivery.

steps.name: Build Empty Digest Payload
steps.description: Constructs the 'nothing today' digest payload when no grants qualify. Receives step_3 false-branch output (which contains step_2's full output including borderline, totalEvaluated, skippedCount). Tracks consecutive empty day count using n8n static data; after 3+ consecutive empty days, escalates with a WARNING-level message. Also propagates deadlineSchemaWarning if present.

output_shape.consecutiveEmptyDays: rolling count of consecutive empty days
output_shape.alertLevel: null or WARNING if 3+ consecutive empty days
output_shape.deadlineSchemaWarning: propagated from step_2 if deadline field drift detected

steps.name: Post Empty Digest to Internal Slack Endpoint
steps.description: POSTs the empty digest 'nothing today' payload to the Slack endpoint. If consecutiveEmptyDays >= 3, the payload includes a WARNING-level escalation message visible to the team.
steps.error_handling: On failure, route to failure alert

steps.name: Build Workflow Failure Alert Payload
steps.description: Constructs an alert payload describing the workflow failure. Uses a Code node (not Set) to properly access error context. Reads error details from $input.first().json and falls back to a descriptive static message rather than relying on $json.error being populated by n8n automatically.

output_shape.error: error description from input context or static fallback

steps.name: Post Failure Alert to Slack
steps.description: POSTs the workflow failure alert to the internal Slack endpoint to notify the team.

gates.note: Workforce Development API response passed forward to next fetch node
Rural Broadband API response passed forward to next fetch node
Veteran Services API response passed forward to next fetch node
Early Childhood Education API response passed forward. step_1e receives all 4 items via $input.all() since each prior node appended its result to the item stream.
true-branch (hasQualifying=true) -> step_4; false-branch (hasQualifying=false) -> step_9 which receives step_2 output via step_3 pass-through
true-branch (blurbsValid=true, including fallback-all with WARNING) -> step_7; false-branch (hard validation failure) -> step_11
step_8 success -> step_10b (reset counter); step_8 failure -> step_11 (alert, counter NOT reset - intentional)

error_handling.on_workflow_failure: Route to step_11 (Build Workflow Failure Alert Payload - Code node) then step_12 (Post Failure Alert to Slack). Failures in step_1/step_1b/step_1c/step_1d (API fetch), step_1e (merge/deduplicate - including all-zero-hits assertion), step_2 (filter - including schema assertion), step_4 (OpenAI blurb generation), step_5/step_6 (quality gate hard failure), step_7 (format), step_8/step_10 (post) all route to the failure alert path. step_8 failure intentionally skips step_10b (counter reset) so the consecutive-empty-days counter is only cleared on confirmed successful Slack delivery.
error_handling.timeout_note: Increased from 300s to 600s to accommodate up to 10 sequential OpenAI calls (each up to 8s) plus 200ms inter-call delays, plus 4 Grants.gov API fetches and Slack POST.
error_handling.merge_node_removal_note: The Merge node (step_1_merge) has been removed per the CRITICAL finding that the Build Agent does not support parallel fan-out/merge wiring. The four HTTP fetch nodes are now wired linearly (step_1 -> step_1b -> step_1c -> step_1d -> step_1e). The step_1e Code node uses $input.all() to read all items accumulated across the sequential chain. This is functionally equivalent for deduplication and validation purposes.

output.format: JSON POST body: { summary: string, items: GrantItem[], borderline: BorderlineItem[], meta: object, isEmpty: boolean, blurbWarning?: string, alertLevel?: string, consecutiveWarning?: string, deadlineSchemaWarning?: string }
output.description: Daily grant digest posted to internal Slack via HTTP endpoint. Qualifying grants include agency, funding ceiling, deadline, Grants.gov link, personalized fit blurb, and blurbSource. Borderline grants listed with missed criteria. Empty days post a 'nothing today' message with escalation WARNING after 3+ consecutive empty days. Full OpenAI outage posts digest with fallback blurbs and WARNING alertLevel (not ERROR). Data pipeline failures post an ERROR alert. Deadline-only schema drift produces a soft deadlineSchemaWarning in the payload.

credentials: OPENAI_API_KEY (environment variable, used in step_4 Code node via $env.OPENAI_API_KEY and $http helper)
Internal Slack endpoint may require Bearer token or HMAC header (add to httpRequest Authorization header if needed)
security.pii_handling: No PII collected or transmitted. Grant data is public federal information. Synopses truncated to 500 chars before sending to OpenAI.
security.data_flow: Grants.gov v2 public API (4 sequential focus-area queries) -> n8n sequential Code node merge/dedup/filter -> OpenAI GPT-4o-mini via $http helper (grant metadata only, no PII) -> internal Slack endpoint

cost_estimate.breakdown: Up to 10 qualifying grants x ~$0.01-0.02 per blurb (GPT-4o-mini at ~150 tokens output + ~200 tokens input per grant). Grants.gov API is free. Internal Slack endpoint is free. 4 sequential API calls per run instead of parallel (no additional cost).

test_cases.name: Happy path - qualifying grants found with blurbs
Empty digest - no grants meet all three criteria
Grants.gov API failure - team alert posted

expected.slack_post_body_summary: WORKFLOW FAILURE: Grants.gov daily digest failed to complete. Manual investigation required.

test_cases.name: Borderline grants present but no fully qualifying grants
OpenAI API failure falls back to template blurb for individual grants (partial failure)
Total OpenAI API failure - all blurbs fallback - digest still posted with WARNING alertLevel (not ERROR)
Grants.gov response envelope unrecognized - schema assertion fires - failure alert sent

expected.slack_post_body_summary: WORKFLOW FAILURE: Grants.gov daily digest failed to complete. Manual investigation required.

test_cases.name: All grants return zero funding ceiling and invalid deadline - schema parse failure - failure alert sent

expected.slack_post_body_summary: WORKFLOW FAILURE: Grants.gov daily digest failed to complete. Manual investigation required.

test_cases.name: All grants have readable ceilings but all deadlines unreadable - soft schema drift warning in payload
All four Grants.gov queries return 0 hits - query parameter mismatch assertion fires - failure alert sent

expected.slack_post_body_summary: WORKFLOW FAILURE: Grants.gov daily digest failed to complete. Manual investigation required.

test_cases.name: Three consecutive empty days - WARNING escalation included in empty digest post
Grant with no oppNumber or id - link falls back to base search URL
More than 10 qualifying grants - capped at 10 blurbs to prevent timeout
Qualifying grants run succeeds - consecutiveEmptyDays counter reset to 0
step_8 Slack POST fails - failure alert sent and counter NOT reset

expected.slack_post_body_summary: WORKFLOW FAILURE: Grants.gov daily digest failed to complete. Manual investigation required.

components_needed: OpenAI API key (GPT-4o-mini access) - stored as OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable
Internal Slack-poster endpoint authentication token (if required) - add to httpRequest Authorization header
Grants.gov v2 API access (public, no key required - confirm rate limits for 4 sequential calls/run)

Verify exact Grants.gov v2 API request body schema before deployment: confirm oppStatuses accepts string array ['forecasted','posted'] and sortBy/sortOrder field names. Run step_1 manually and inspect response envelope to confirm extractHits() field names.
Verify exact Grants.gov v2 API response envelope field names before deployment (run step_1e and inspect firstGrantKeys in output)
Verify Grants.gov detail page URL format for oppNumber-based deep links
n8n instance must support $http.request() in Code nodes (available in n8n >= 0.187 with the Code node). Verify $http helper availability on deployed instance.
n8n static data ($getWorkflowStaticData) must be enabled on the instance for consecutive-empty-days tracking to persist across runs
Confirm global_timeout_seconds=600 is within n8n instance execution timeout limits

ARCHITECTURE NOTE: The four Grants.gov HTTP fetch nodes (step_1 through step_1d) are wired linearly rather than in parallel due to Build Agent constraint: parallel fan-out/merge via Merge node is not supported. The step_1e Code node reads all 4 items via $input.all() from the sequential chain. This means the 4 API calls execute sequentially (not concurrently) adding ~4-8s to total runtime, which is acceptable within the 600s timeout.
